report_name: New Multi-Stage Trojan Targets Visual Studio Projects in Supply Chain Attack

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The Trojan compromises Visual Studio project files, a component of the software development environment, to inject malicious code into the final application during the build process.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
attack_patterns.description: The Trojan likely uses a custom MSBuild `<Exec>` task within a project file to run a malicious command or script during the build process.

attack_patterns.name: Code Signing
attack_patterns.description: The trojanized application, once compiled, is implicitly signed with the legitimate developer's code signing certificate, making it appear trustworthy to end-users and security products.

attack_patterns.name: MSBuild
attack_patterns.description: The Trojan abuses MSBuild by adding custom build tasks to project files, such as using the `<Exec>` task to run malicious commands during the build process.

malware.name: Visual Studio Supply Chain Trojan
malware.description: A new multi-stage Trojan that infiltrates Microsoft Visual Studio projects to inject malicious code into legitimate applications during the build process, facilitating software supply chain attacks.

tools.name: Microsoft Visual Studio
tools.description: The targeted integrated development environment (IDE). The Trojan infiltrates Visual Studio projects to conduct supply chain attacks.

tools.name: MSBuild
tools.description: The build platform for Visual Studio, which is abused by the Trojan to execute arbitrary code or inject malicious source code by modifying project files with custom build tasks like `<Exec>`.

identities.name: Software Developers
identities.description: The primary target of the Trojan, whose workstations and Visual Studio projects are compromised to initiate the supply chain attack.

indicators.name: Suspicious MSBuild Child Process
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious MSBuild Child Process
id: 4f8a1b2c-3d4e-4f5a-8b9c-0d1e2f3a4b5c
status: experimental
description: Detects the MSBuild process spawning network-related utilities or other suspicious tools. This is anomalous behavior for a compiler process and may indicate a compromised build environment, such as the one targeted by the Visual Studio Supply Chain Trojan.
references:
  - https://cybersecuritynews.com/new-trojan-turns-visual-studio-projects-into-a-software-supply-chain-attack-vector/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
  - attack.t1127.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    ParentImage|endswith: '\msbuild.exe'
    Image|endswith:
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\wget.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the MSBuild process spawning unusual child processes, which is anomalous behavior for a compiler process and may indicate a compromised build environment.

indicators.name: MSBuild Initiating Outbound Network Connections
indicators.pattern:
title: MSBuild Initiating Outbound Network Connections
id: 9a8b7c6d-5e4f-4a3b-8c2d-1e0f9a8b7c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the MSBuild process initiating outbound network connections. This is highly anomalous behavior for a compiler process and may indicate a compromised build environment, such as one targeted by the Visual Studio Supply Chain Trojan.
references:
  - https://cybersecuritynews.com/new-trojan-turns-visual-studio-projects-into-a-software-supply-chain-attack-vector/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
  - attack.command_and_control
level: high
logsource:
  category: network_connection
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\msbuild.exe'
    Initiated: true
  filter_local:
    DestinationIp|in:
      - '127.0.0.1'
      - '::1'
  condition: selection and not filter_local
indicators.description: Detects the MSBuild process initiating outbound network connections, which is highly anomalous behavior for a compiler process.

indicators.name: Suspicious MSBuild Project File Modification
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious MSBuild Project File Modification
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-4a9b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: Monitors for modifications to MSBuild project files (.csproj, .vbproj) by processes other than common developer tools. This can be a vector for supply chain attacks like the Visual Studio Supply Chain Trojan.
references:
  - https://cybersecuritynews.com/new-trojan-turns-visual-studio-projects-into-a-software-supply-chain-attack-vector/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-12
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.csproj'
      - '.vbproj'
      - 'Directory.Build.props'
  filter_legit_procs:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\devenv.exe'
      - '\msbuild.exe'
      - '\git.exe'
      - '\Code.exe'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit_procs
indicators.description: Detects modifications to MSBuild project files by processes other than legitimate developer tools, which could indicate tampering.
